summary: A vibrant 1975 audience/matrix tape captures a high‑energy show highlighted by a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s and standout Donna Jean vocals.
review: The recording, sourced from the audience with matrix elements, is praised for its clarity, punchy mix and authentic crowd ambience. Musically the concert shines on the second‑set sequence Help > Slip > Franklin’s, big‑river energy and a fiery Donna Jean performance, though a few vocal slips by Jerry and a mediocre Truckin' keep it from being flawless.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (9/10) give 4‑5 stars, praising both the performance and the sound quality; only one outlier is negative. Weighted rating (70% show, 30% recording) yields ~4.1.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several reviewers call it a great/crisp audience tape; one notes a "great matrix" mix, describing the sound as balanced, punchy and full of crowd ambience with no major drop‑outs.

standout_songs: Goin' Down The Road Feelin' Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert builds strong momentum into the second set, with Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s serving as a centerpiece. Energy stays high through Big River and GDTRFB, though the latter part of the show shows signs of fatigue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed – praised for guitar tone but criticized for several vocal lyric slips, especially on Help and later songs.
band_member_comments.Phil: Positive – noted as bringing solid bass energy throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Positive – highlighted for strong vocal chemistry with Donna Jean and tight harmonies.
band_member_comments.Keys: Positive – Keith Godchaux described as "spot on" and adding lush keyboard texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally solid; reviewer notes drummers were steady but historically could be indulgent or rush tempo.
